name: Image

aliases.name: Image
aliases.comment: Data Model representing Image data, that can be used to create image, or convert primitives to use image pixels as data

values.name: decode
values.comment:
Convert blob of image (`png` or `bmp`) into [`Image`](#Image)

    import Http

    type Msg
        = GotImage (Result Http.Error (Maybe Image))

    getImage : Cmd Msg
    getImage =
        Http.get
            { url = "/image.png"
            , expect = Http.expectBytes GotImage Image.decode
            }

values.name: dimensions
values.comment: Get `width` and `height` of [`Image`](#Image)

values.name: fromArray
values.comment: Create [`Image`](#Image) of `Array Int` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: fromArray2d
values.comment: Create [`Image`](#Image) of `Array (Array Pixel)` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Create [`Image`](#Image) of `List Int` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: fromList2d
values.comment: Create [`Image`](#Image) of `List (List Int)` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: toArray
values.comment: Take [`Image`](#Image) of and converts it to `Array Pixel` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: toArray2d
values.comment: Take [`Image`](#Image) of and converts it to matrix `Array (Array Pixel)` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: toBmp
values.comment:
The BMP file format, also known as bitmap image file or device independent bitmap (DIB) file format or simply a bitmap, is a raster graphics image file format used to store bitmap digital images, independently of the display device (such as a graphics adapter), especially on Microsoft Windows and OS/2 operating systems.

**Note**: Using BMP 32bit is discouraged due to lack of proper support across browsers, so image will be 24bit (no alpha channel)

values.name: toBmpUrl
values.comment: Create base64-url that can be used directly as img source (`img [ src <| toBmpUrl myImage ]`)

values.name: toList
values.comment: Take [`Image`](#Image) of and converts it to `List Pixel` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: toList2d
values.comment: Take [`Image`](#Image) of and converts it to matrix `List (List Pixel)` where each `Pixel` is `Int` as `0xRRGGBBAA`

values.name: toPng
values.comment:
Portable Network Graphics (PNG) is a raster-graphics file-format that supports lossless data compression. PNG was developed as an improved, non-patented replacement for Graphics Interchange Format (GIF).

PNG supports palette-based images (with palettes of 24-bit RGB or 32-bit RGBA colors), grayscale images (with or without alpha channel for transparency), and full-color non-palette-based RGB images (with or without alpha channel).

values.name: toPngUrl
values.comment: Create base64-url that can be used directly as img source (`img [ src <| toPngUrl myImage ]`)

name: Image.Advanced

values.name: eval
values.comment:
When decoding images they are decoded in "lazy way" (real decoding is postponed until data is needed)
this function do decode, useful if you need to encode multiple images from same source

values.name: info
values.comment: Get image info

values.name: map
values.comment: Apply a function on every pixel in an image.

values.name: toBmp24
values.comment: Encode image into BMP24

values.name: toBmp32
values.comment:
Encode image into BMP32

**Note**: Using BMP 32bit is discouraged due to lack of proper support across browsers

values.name: toPng32
values.comment: Encode image into True color with alpha PNG image

aliases.type: { width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int, fileSize : Basics.Int, pixelStart : Basics.Int, dibHeader : Basics.Int, color_planes : Basics.Int, bitsPerPixel : Image.Info.BmpBitsPerPixel, compression : Basics.Int, dataSize : Basics.Int }
